title: Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room
summary: An indoor exposure-reduction plan for ashfall that closes air entry, protects breathing and eyes, limits driving, separates dirty clothing, and avoids dangerous roof or electrical cleanup.
content:
An indoor exposure-reduction plan for ashfall that closes air entry, protects breathing and eyes, limits driving, separates dirty clothing, and avoids dangerous roof or electrical cleanup.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: plastic sheeting and tape for obvious leaks
indoor water food medicines and radio
steps: Follow official ash and evacuation zones; shelter indoors only when authorities do not order evacuation and the building is structurally safe.
Close windows and doors, reduce outside-air intake where safe, choose an interior room, and keep pets inside with protected food and water.
When brief outdoor movement is unavoidable, cover skin and eyes, use appropriate respiratory protection, move slowly, and remove dusty outer layers at the entrance.
Clean with low-dust methods after official advice; lightly dampen suitable surfaces, protect electronics and drains, and use qualified help for roofs, wiring, engines, or heavy deposits.
warnings: Do not use leaf blowers, dry sweeping, or compressed air that resuspends ash.
Do not climb onto an ash-loaded roof or drive in heavy ash unless authorities direct evacuation.
common_mistakes: Starting Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Volcanic ash shelter and clean-air room.']
